facts.text: Dallas was founded at a "white rock crossing" of the Trinity River, where wagons could easily cross before bridges existed.
The city’s name probably honors Vice President George Mifflin Dallas, who served the United States from 1845 to 1849.
A 3.5‑mile stretch of former Missouri‑Kansas‑Texas Railroad was turned into Dallas’s premier trail, preserving a piece of rail history.
Deep Ellum, just east of downtown, became the South’s jazz and blues hotspot in the 1920s and 1930s and still echoes that legacy.
The Dallas Morning News, the city’s major daily newspaper, began publishing in 1885 and has been a staple of local journalism ever since.
The Dallas Cowboys, who play at AT&T Stadium, are one of the city’s most famous sports teams and have won five Super Bowls.
